# Purpose
The Apache License, Version 2.0, is a legal document that outlines the terms and conditions for the use, reproduction, and distribution of software. It defines key terms such as "License," "Licensor," "Contributor," and "Derivative Works," which are essential for understanding the rights and responsibilities of parties involved. The license grants users a perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use the software, create derivative works, and distribute copies, provided they comply with specified conditions. It also includes provisions for patent rights, redistribution requirements, and disclaimers of warranty and liability. The document concludes with instructions on how to apply the Apache License to a work, ensuring that users can properly attribute and license their contributions.
# Content Summary
The document is the Apache License, Version 2.0, which outlines the terms and conditions for using, reproducing, and distributing software. It defines key terms such as "License," "Licensor," "Legal Entity," "You," "Source," "Object," "Work," "Derivative Works," "Contribution," and "Contributor." These definitions establish the framework for understanding the rights and responsibilities of parties involved with the software.

The license grants users a perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive, royalty-free copyright and patent license to use, reproduce, prepare derivative works, publicly display, perform, sublicense, and distribute the software and its derivative works. It specifies conditions for redistribution, including providing a copy of the license, marking modified files, retaining notices, and including a NOTICE file if applicable.

Contributions submitted to the Licensor are under the terms of this license unless otherwise stated. The license does not grant rights to use the Licensor's trademarks, except for describing the origin of the work. It includes disclaimers of warranty and limitations of liability, stating that the software is provided "AS IS" without warranties or conditions of any kind. Users are responsible for determining the appropriateness of using or redistributing the software and assume any associated risks.

The license allows users to offer support, warranty, indemnity, or other liability obligations when redistributing the software, but they must do so on their own behalf and indemnify other contributors. The appendix provides instructions on how to apply the Apache License to a work, including a boilerplate notice for identifying information.
